inline_comments.message: This appears to modify behavior, without much explanation

Hi Steve,
The method I changed assumes to choose NorthBoundRequest object by: ACTION, REQUEST_SCOPE, IS_ALACARTE and CLOUD_OWNER. 
But to this query there is additional condition: where serviceType="*". 

So the first question is why serviceType is taking into account here?. There is another method that takes serviceType additionally, and it is named getNorthBoundRequestByActionAndIsALaCarteAndRequestScopeAndCloudOwnerAndServiceType. 

Secondly, why asterix "*"? I can not see any place in the code that would set this field as "*". 
I think that maybe somebody thought that "*" means "any" here, but it does not work that way in sql.

please respond Steve

We are currently just using * to mean a wild card, I understand it will not do that in sql.  I am fine with refactoring this later, the least impacting fix would be to simply mark the columns with the wild card.  Service Type will allow us to control the flow for different service types.
